purpose: Theme constants (status/level/timeline color maps) plus initStatusColorsFromCSS() that pulls the canonical theme from CSS custom properties at runtime. Exposes STATUS_COLORS, STATUS_BG_COLORS, LEVEL_COLORS, LEVEL_BG_COLORS, TIMELINE_COLORS for direct import; also a colorWithAlpha(color, alpha) helper that handles rgba/rgb/hex inputs. App.jsx calls initStatusColorsFromCSS() once on mount to sync these maps with --color-completed, --color-in-progress, etc., from the active theme.

exports.name: getDashboardLabel
exports.signature: (id) => string — backwards compat for legacy dashboardN, otherwise uppercase hex

exports.name: initStatusColorsFromCSS
exports.signature: () => void — must be called once after CSS loaded

gotchas: Constants are MUTATED by initStatusColorsFromCSS — they're not frozen. Direct imports give you the LATEST values only if init has run; reading them at module-load time gives stale defaults.

Theme switching at runtime requires re-calling initStatusColorsFromCSS() AFTER the data-theme attribute change — currently App.jsx only calls once on mount, so switching themes does not refresh status colors.
STATUS_BG_COLORS.pending uses --surface override (NOT alpha-derived from STATUS_COLORS.pending) — it's intentionally bound to the panel surface for the 'no-progress' empty state.
LEVEL_COLORS.info is overridden by --color-accent if available, otherwise --color-in-progress — small mismatch with hardcoded purple default.
getDashboardLabel returns 'Dashboard N' for legacy 'dashboardN' IDs but UPPERCASE hex for new short IDs — UI must handle both forms.
colorWithAlpha falls through and returns the original color UNCHANGED if it doesn't match rgba/rgb/hex — no error thrown.
patterns: Theme tokens read from CSS custom properties via getComputedStyle(documentElement).getPropertyValue — gives JS access to CSS-defined theme without duplication.
Mutable constant maps initialized once per app boot — preferred over re-deriving on every component render.
conventions: Status keys: completed, in_progress, pending, failed, claimed, blocked.
Log level keys: info, warn, error, debug, permission, deviation.
